decisions.rationale: Built first then abandoned — can remove in cleanup

decisions.decision: User info stored in sessionStorage not localStorage
decisions.rationale: Cleared on tab close — better security default
sessionStorage lost on tab reopen; saved credentials in SQLite remain
Simpler setup — no DB server needed

next_action: docker compose restart backend → open http://localhost:3000 → click Continue with Atlassian → enter real Jira credentials → confirm dashboard shows user name/email/avatar
context_notes: Session goal was making Jira connection real and verifiable. OAuth 3LO built first then abandoned for simpler Basic Auth modal. All backend auth logic was already in place (jira_service.py) — only needed modal UI + user info passthrough. Dashboard now proves connection worked by showing Jira user identity.
